subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference
- **Standard game** — 4 to 20+ players.
- **Recommended** — 7 players.

#### Core Components
- **Black cards** — 100 prompt/question cards with fill-in-the-blank statements or questions. Some require 1 white card response; others require 2 ("Pick 2") or 3 ("Pick 3").
- **White cards** — 500 answer cards with nouns, phrases, or concepts.
- **Rulebook** — 1 brief rulebook.

#### Card Details
- **Black cards** — Each has a statement with one or more blanks, or a question. The number of blanks determines how many white cards must be played.
- **White cards** — Each has a single answer, noun, or phrase. All white text on white background.
- **Pick 2 / Pick 3** — Some black cards are marked "Pick 2" or "Pick 3" at the bottom, requiring multiple white card submissions.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Shuffle black cards** — Shuffle the black card deck and place face-down in the center.
2. **Shuffle white cards** — Shuffle the white card deck and place nearby.
3. **Deal white cards** — Deal 10 white cards to each player. Players may look at their own cards.
4. **Select first Card Czar** — Choose the first Card Czar by any method (the rules suggest whoever most recently defecated).

#### Table Layout
- **Black deck** — Center of table, face-down.
- **White deck** — Draw pile nearby.
- **Submission area** — Clear space for face-down white card submissions.
- **Awesome Points pile** — Each player's won black cards stacked in front of them.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Roles
- **Card Czar** — 1 player per round; rotates clockwise.
- **Players** — All other players submit white cards.

#### Hand Management
- **Starting hand** — 10 white cards per player.
- **Replenishment** — After each round, draw back up to 10 white cards.
- **Czar's hand** — The Card Czar keeps their hand but does not play white cards during their Czar turn.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-First-Turn Checks
- **All players have 10 white cards** — Verify hand size.
- **Card Czar identified** — First Czar is selected.
- **Decks accessible** — Both card piles are shuffled and placed.

#### First Action
1. **Czar draws black card** — The Card Czar draws the top black card and reads it aloud.
2. **Players submit** — All other players choose white card(s) from their hand to fill in the blank(s).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Flow
1. **Draw black card** — Card Czar draws and reads a black card aloud.
2. **Check Pick count** — Note if the card says "Pick 2" or "Pick 3" (default is Pick 1).
3. **Submit white cards** — All other players place their white card(s) face-down. For Pick 2/3, cards are submitted in the order they should be read.
4. **Shuffle submissions** — Card Czar collects and shuffles all submissions (keeping multi-card sets together).
5. **Read combinations** — Card Czar reads the black card with each player's white card(s) inserted, re-reading the black card each time.
6. **Pick winner** — Card Czar selects the funniest combination.
7. **Award Awesome Point** — Winning player takes the black card as 1 Awesome Point.
8. **Draw up** — All players who submitted draw white cards to return to 10.
9. **Rotate Czar** — Next player clockwise becomes the Card Czar.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Player Actions
- **Select white card(s)** — Choose from your hand the card(s) that create the funniest combination with the black card.
- **Submit face-down** — Place cards face-down to maintain anonymity.
- **Order matters** — For Pick 2/3, the order you submit your cards is the order they will be read.
- **Draw replacements** — After judging, draw back to 10 cards.

#### Card Czar Actions
- **Read black card** — Read aloud clearly.
- **Read all combos** — Re-read the black card with each submission for fair comparison.
- **Pick favorite** — Select the funniest or most outrageous combination.
- **Czar is final** — No appeals or overrides.

#### Pick 2 / Pick 3 Rules
- **Multiple blanks** — Submit the exact number of white cards indicated.
- **Reading order** — Cards are read in the order submitted (first card fills first blank).
- **Draw extra** — Draw enough white cards after the round to return to 10.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### House Rules (Official Variants)
- **Happy Ending** — When ready to stop, play the "Make a Haiku" black card as the final round.
- **Rebooting the Universe** — At any time, players may trade in an Awesome Point to discard their entire hand and draw 10 new white cards.
- **Packing Heat** — For Pick 2, draw an extra white card before submitting (hand temporarily goes to 12, then back to 10).
- **Rando Cardrissian** — Each round, pick a random white card from the deck as an additional submission. If Rando wins, all players lose.
- **God Is Dead** — Remove the Card Czar. All players submit, then vote on the best combo. No one may vote for their own.
- **Survival of the Fittest** — Remove the Czar. All players submit, then each round has 2 submissions eliminated by group vote until one remains.
- **Serious Business** — Instead of picking funniest, Card Czar picks the submission they find most genuinely disturbing.
- **Never Have I Ever** — At any point, discard cards you don't understand and draw replacements with no penalty.

#### Adult Content
- **Mature humor** — The game is explicitly designed for adults (17+) and contains offensive, sexual, and dark humor.
- **Content advisory** — Some players may want to pre-screen and remove specific cards.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **Point target** — Play to an agreed number of Awesome Points (commonly 5-10 depending on group size and time).
- **Time limit** — Alternatively, play for a set amount of time and count points at the end.
- **Card exhaustion** — Game may end when the black card deck runs out.

#### Scoring Model
- **Awesome Points** — Each black card won = 1 Awesome Point.
- **Most points wins** — Player with the most Awesome Points at game end wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **No official tiebreaker** — In case of a tie, play a sudden-death round or declare a shared victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Know the Czar
- **Humor profiling** — Track what makes each Czar laugh. Some prefer shock value, others prefer clever wordplay.
- **Adapt per Czar** — Switch between dark, absurd, and clever depending on who is judging.
- **First-round read** — The first few rounds establish each player's judging patterns.

#### Hand Management
- **Save power cards** — Cards that are universally funny or shocking should be saved for the right Czar.
- **Burn duds** — Play your weakest cards when the Czar's taste is hard to predict or when the black card is mediocre.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Always going dark** — Maximum shock value loses its punch after several rounds. Variety wins.
- **Ignoring the Czar** — Playing what you think is funny rather than what the current Czar finds funny.
- **Random play** — Throwing down a card without reading the black card carefully. Fit matters.
- **Wrong order for Pick 2** — The order of your two white cards changes the joke entirely. Think about the reading flow.
- **Hoarding** — Saving great cards too long. The perfect black card may never come, and your hand stagnates.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Shock vs. Clever
- **Shock** — Go for maximum outrageousness. Works well with certain Czars and early in the game.
- **Clever** — Go for wordplay or unexpected wit. Works better with experienced groups and later in the night.

#### Decision 2: Rebooting Your Hand
- **Reboot** — Spend an Awesome Point to get 10 fresh cards if your hand is all duds.
- **Hold** — Keep your point if you have even a few usable cards. Points win the game.

#### Decision 3: Reading as Czar
- **Dramatic reading** — Sell every submission with energy. Makes your Czar round more fun for everyone.
- **Deadpan reading** — Let the humor speak for itself. Works well for absurd or dark submissions.
